title: crataegus × palmstruchii var. lepida
intro: Crataegus × palmstruchii var. lepida, also known as Lepida Hawthorn, is a hybrid plant species within the hawthorn family (Crataegus spp.) that is native to parts of Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its cardiovascular benefits, and is used to help lower blood pressure, improve blood circulation, and reduce the risk of heart disease. The leaves and fruits of Lepida Hawthorn are traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions that are said to have a range of health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal medicine, Lepida Hawthorn is often used to support heart health and is considered a natural remedy for conditions such as high blood pressure, angina, and cardiovascular disease.
